mapping.a_element: overdamped diffusion process driven out of equilibrium (the underlying Langevin/SDE dynamics)
mapping.b_element: the stochastic differential equation / Ito process (the underlying stochastic dynamics)

mapping.a_element: close-to-equilibrium Boltzmann structure underlying entropy-production principles
mapping.b_element: the built-in Boltzmann-Gibbs distribution and potential function of the new interpretation

brief_justification: Both papers concern overdamped stochastic diffusions with a Boltzmann-Gibbs / potential-function stationary structure, but their transferable machinery differs: A applies steady-state large-deviation/fluctuation analysis, decomposing joint occupation-time and current statistics into a time-symmetric 'traffic' sector and a time-antisymmetric entropy-production sector, whereas B works in the calculus of stochastic-integration conventions, deriving the drift relation between a new SDE interpretation and the Ito process. The shared object (Langevin diffusion) is the domain, not a shared operational method, and no role-for-role correspondence between fluctuation functionals and interpretation-conversion formulas holds.
